Uzbekistan.status: ELIMINATED — worst GD in the group (-7)

matches._note: PRIOR LESSON, SAME PLAYER (Shomurodov), SAME 2H-SOT QUESTION: POR-UZB Q5 confirmed RULE15 cleanly — Shomurodov got 0 2H SOT in the 5-0 blowout, our 0.25 vs crowd 0.35 won +8.78. Also: Uzbekistan's offside count in that match (2) came from abandoning their compact shape once the blowout was total — 'the blowout reversal rule DOES apply' and the explicit standing floor is 'never go below 0.25 on any 2+ offsides question.'

context_flags: ROLE_REVERSAL_FROM_COL_CDR: in COL-CDR (last match), DR Congo was the extreme underdog and RULE15 correctly suppressed Bakambu's SOT (+10.45 win). TODAY DR Congo is the FAVORITE (58.8%) — RULE15 does NOT apply to Bakambu this time; price him on his own merit as the front man of the stronger side.

UZB_BLOWOUT_RISK_BUT_LESS_EXTREME: DR Congo's 58.8% favorite status is real but far less extreme than Portugal's 5-0 demolition — apply the Shomurodov/offside lessons from POR-UZB with some moderation, not at full blowout intensity.
UZB_TERMINAL_FORM: 0 points, -7 goal difference, has not scored against a top-tier opponent (0 in 2 of their last context windows) — among the weakest remaining teams in the tournament.

questions.text: Will Uzbekistan commit more fouls than DR Congo?
questions.reasoning: Raw normal-approx off ESPN averages (UZB 14.5 vs CDR 13.0) gives 0.64. DR Congo being the favorite today should mean more possession for them, pushing Uzbekistan further into a defending role and reinforcing (not contradicting) the foul direction.

questions.text: Will Uzbekistan be caught offside 2 or more times?
questions.reasoning: UZB offsides: 0 (vs COL) and 2 (vs POR, in their 5-0 blowout). Raw Poisson at λ=1.0 gives only 0.26, but the explicit standing lesson from POR-UZB is 'never go below 0.25 on any 2+ offsides question' AND that blowouts make a low-block team abandon shape and push forward, raising offside risk. DR Congo (58.8% favorite) is a real but more moderate mismatch than Portugal's was — set above the raw number and the floor, but below the extreme-blowout level.

questions.text: Will both teams have at least 1 shot on target in the second half?
questions.reasoning: Half-split Poisson off full-match ESPN averages (CDR λ_2H=0.75→P(≥1)=0.53; UZB λ_2H=1.0→P(≥1)=0.63). Joint=0.33.

questions.text: Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
questions.reasoning: Direct compound market: Yes/Over=32.05%. Trust it — both teams have shown limited combined scoring output this tournament outside of facing Portugal.

questions.text: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown?
questions.reasoning: Direct compound market: Penalty Yes=25.35%, Any sent off Yes=14.74%. P(OR)=1-(0.7465×0.8526)=0.3633.

questions.text: Will Uzbekistan have more shots on target than DR Congo in the second half?
questions.reasoning: Half-split Skellam (UZB λ_2H=1.0, CDR λ_2H=0.75) gives 0.40 raw. Shaded down per the POR-UZB lesson that this team's attacking output collapses specifically against stronger opposition, even though today's mismatch is less extreme than that 5-0 game.

questions.text: Will Eldor Shomurodov have at least 1 shot on target in the second half?
questions.reasoning: Same player, same exact question as POR-UZB Q5, where RULE15 was directly confirmed (0 2H SOT, our 0.25 beat crowd's 0.35 by +8.78). The naive half-split off today's market-implied full-match rate (0.522→λ_2H≈0.37→P(≥1)=0.31) doesn't account for the established pattern that this specific player's shot output collapses further than a naive split suggests once his team is behind. DR Congo is a real, if more moderate, favorite — shade below the naive split but not as far down as the 5-0 blowout case.

questions.text: Will DR Congo win the match?
questions.reasoning: Smarkets FTR: Congo DR 58.82%. Direct tier-1 market.

questions.text: Will Cédric Bakambu score or assist a goal (excluding own goals)?
questions.pick_tier: MEDIUM-HIGH CONFIDENCE — ROLE REVERSED FROM LAST MATCH
questions.reasoning: Illiquid full-match offer (0.4673, adjusted ×0.945=0.442). Unlike COL-CDR (where Bakambu's SOT was correctly suppressed by RULE15 as the extreme underdog's forward), DR Congo is the favorite today — no RULE15 discount applies. Trust the market near-raw as Bakambu leads the attack of the stronger side.

questions.text: Will Uzbekistan have 5 or more corner kicks?
questions.reasoning: Direct market: Uzbekistan Over4.5 corners = 32.40%. Consistent with their generally low corner output (3, then 2) and reduced possession share expected against a favored DR Congo.
